prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Interview me about the top three difficulties I see in our team's Claude rollout

risks_fired.evidence: push back once: ask for a specific example, or ask whether that's the actual difficulty or a symptom of something deeper
risks_fired.fix_hint: Strengthen the push-back instruction to 'name the deeper cause and ask which is the real difficulty' — less room to land softly.

risks_fired.evidence: 4 named structural steps (start with question, push back, ask name, save); no preamble suppressor
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'No preamble. Start with: "What's the first difficulty you see?"' as the prompt's last line.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: I have a few ideas about what we should do next with Claude rollout.  Interview

risks_fired.evidence: capture those ideas clearly in my `rollout-notes.md` — no append/overwrite instruction; file already contains Phase 1 interview content
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'Append the ideas to rollout-notes.md — do not overwrite existing content.' to name the integration mode explicitly.

risks_fired.evidence: Show me the proposed addition in chat before saving it — pre-confirm pattern may produce a hesitate-and-ask loop after each idea
risks_fired.fix_hint: Replace with 'Tell me what you added after saving it' to use post-action-report shape and eliminate the confirm loop.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Create our group's rollout synthesis.  First ask me for the exact folder names o

risks_fired.evidence: 7+ named sequential sub-steps with no in-fence 'No preamble.' suppressor and no rule-17-compliant body primer post-fence
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'No preamble. Ask me for the folder names first.' as the prompt's last line, OR add a body primer immediately after the fence naming the preamble + skim-past cue.

risks_fired.evidence: Propose the crux. Use Richard Rumelt's meaning: the one difficulty that, if our team got it right, would make the others follow — no explicit single-commitment instruction
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'Name exactly ONE crux. Do not offer alternatives. The group will push back if you are wrong.' before the show-in-chat step.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Based on what you have read, list the other possible cruxes.

risks_fired.evidence: Based on what you have read — relies on retained reading context; may inflate or invent if session has compacted
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'only from the rollout notes you read in this session; do not invent' to anchor to evidence.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Confirm the rollout synthesis file exists in `shared/`. If it does not exist, sa

risks_fired.evidence: 5 named sequential sub-steps (confirm, read synthesis, scan notes, ask group, append+read+save+summarise); no in-fence suppressor; no rule-17-compliant body primer
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'No preamble. Check shared/ first.' as the prompt's last line, or add a one-sentence body primer after the fence.

risks_fired.evidence: add your brief read below them: which idea best fits the crux, which might be premature — 'might be premature' allows hedged non-committal reads
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'if you cannot distinguish, name the weakest fit explicitly anyway' to force a concrete call.

risks_fired.evidence: which might be premature — Claude may rate every idea as useful and avoid naming a clear weak fit

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Use the same group name from the synthesis file you just saved. If you cannot te

risks_fired.evidence: 4 numbered cycle steps with 6+ nested sub-items; no in-fence 'No preamble.' suppressor and no post-fence body primer meeting rule-17 structural trigger shape
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'No preamble. Start cycle 1 now.' as the prompt's last line, or add a body primer: 'Claude will narrate before acting; skip to the first action line.'

risks_fired.evidence: one comment or sharper question from our group — 'sharper question' may soften to a gentle observation rather than a pointed challenge of the crux selection
risks_fired.fix_hint: Strengthen to 'one pointed question that challenges whether the crux they named is the real constraint' rather than 'one comment or sharper question'.

risks_fired.evidence: append a section named with our group name … save the file — explicit append verb mitigates; low risk on repeated re-visits within same loop
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'do not modify existing sections' to the append instruction if concerned about repeated cycle visits to the same file.
